record.text: Channel9 Next-Level SQL in VS Code: GitHub Copilot Custom Instructions, Plan Mode & Skills | Data Exposed: GitHub Copilot already generates awesome SQL code but awesome is different from aligned with your team. In this episode, we explore how to take full control of… #SQL #VScode #GitHubCopilot

external.description:
GitHub Copilot already generates awesome SQL code but awesome is different from aligned with your team. In this episode, we explore how to take full control of GitHub Copilot context inside VS Code using the MSSQL extension. 

We start with a clean workspace to demonstrate what GitHub Copilot produces with zero context, then add a single custom instruction file that teaches it your team's exact T-SQL conventions automatically. We then use Plan Mode to design a full data model from a PRD (Product Requirements Document) and bring it to life in Schema Designer with Agent Mode, connecting GitHub Copilot to your actual database. From there, we create a skills file that teaches GitHub Copilot your vector search architecture as a slash command, generating SQL Server 2025/Azure SQL Database T-SQL without explaining anything in the prompt. To wrap up, we open the GitHub Copilot debug panel to inspect the full LLM request payload, including the system prompt and injected context, so you can see exactly what GitHub Copilot receives with every request.

Learn how to make GitHub Copilot and the MSSQL extension for VS Code work like a teammate who already knows your standards and architecture in this episode of Data Exposed.

record.text:
Cheapest defense: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, .github/copilot-instructions.md under CODEOWNERS. Five-minute change.

Full writeup: brooksmcmillin.com/blog/poisoni...

Other things that slip through:
- pickle.loads(blob) with a docstring claiming a sealed admin container
- SSRF behind a "patched in 2.4.1" release note the agent fetched
- Auth bypass buried in a 2,000-line PR
- .cursorrules inside node_modules poisoning every future review

The pattern that works: declarative beats directive.

"Do not flag X" gets caught. "X is mitigated at layer Y, per ADR-0042" gets believed. The model defers to documentation instead of resisting an order. ADR-0042 doesn't exist. The ticket number is enough.

The meeting aims to reinforce the strategic partnership between Russia and China, which has deepened following Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Both leaders exchanged congratulatory letters marking 30 years of their countries' strategic partnership, emphasising growing trade and diplomatic ties. Moscow remains economically reliant on Beijing, particularly as China continues to purchase Russian oil amidst international sanctions. Unlike Trump's visit, which was described as high on symbolism but low on concrete results, the Putin-Xi summit is expected to highlight the stability of their long-standing bilateral relationship. Analysts suggest that Xi will brief Putin on his discussions with Trump, ensuring Moscow that China has not entered agreements that could disadvantage Russian interests. Energy and trade are likely to dominate the discussions, as Russia seeks to maintain Chinese support in sustaining its war effort in Ukraine, while China remains focused on securing energy resources and regional stability. The visit underscores the importance of Sino-Russian ties in the current global geopolitical landscape.

Active primarily during the 1940s and 1950s, the label became celebrated for its sophisticated architectural cutting, masterful draping techniques, and fluid yet structured silhouettes that emphasized femininity without heavy reliance on restrictive corsetry. Their designs often featured intricate pleating, bold sweeping lines, and high-quality craftsmanship that stood out in the post-war Parisian fashion scene. The house's creations were regularly featured in prestigious publications such as Vogue and L’Officiel, showcasing elegant daywear, suits, coats, and evening gowns. Notable examples include rose satin dresses with dramatic shoulder drapery, rabbit fur coats mimicking ocelot patterns, black wool afternoon dresses with cape effects, and various jersey and tweed suits tailored with precision. Models like Barbara Goalen, Sophie Malgat, Ghislaine Arsac, and others wore these pieces in professional photoshoots. The partnership concluded in late 1957 when Mad Maltezos departed to join Patou. Mad Carpentier represented the pinnacle of French tailoring and creativity during a period of recovery and elegance in European fashion, highlighting innovative use of fabrics like wool, silk, satin, and prints while maintaining a focus on wearable sophistication and structural beauty.

external.description: In 1985, before Cindy Crawford became a global icon, she was studying chemical engineering at Northwestern University and modeling in Chicago. Photographer Barry Elz recognized her potential early and frequently collaborated with her, capturing images that showcased her natural beauty and helped her portfolio stand out. These studio photographs reflect the mid-1980s shift in fashion, moving away from delicate, 'waif-like' models toward stronger, athletic, and curvier figures—a style that Crawford would eventually epitomize. The high-contrast black and white images, often retaining film borders, have a raw, test-shoot quality, bridging the gap between commercial catalog work and high-fashion editorial photography. Interestingly, while some editors of that era would airbrush out Crawford’s signature beauty mark, Elz preserved it, highlighting her distinctive features. This collection offers both a glimpse into Crawford’s early career and a snapshot of the evolving fashion aesthetics of the mid-1980s.
external.title: Early Studio Photos of Cindy Crawford by Barry Elz in 1985

external.description: In a historic meeting in Beijing, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping signaled a new approach to international relations, moving away from the traditional narrative of rivalry or partnership. Rather than announcing trade deals or joint statements, the summit highlighted a mutual understanding: both nations, as structurally interdependent superpowers, must manage their competition without letting it escalate into conflict. Xi introduced a framework he called a “constructive China-US relationship of strategic stability,” emphasizing cooperation, moderate competition, manageable differences, and promises of peace. Trump endorsed this vision, calling the bilateral relationship “the G2” and framing it as historically consequential. Both leaders acknowledged the global strategic and economic stakes, with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as an immediate test of cooperation. The summit reflects a shift from black-and-white Cold War thinking to a more nuanced approach, prioritizing pragmatic management of trade, energy, technology, and security issues. Taiwan remains a critical red line, underscoring the fragility of this managed order. For the rest of the world, including middle powers like Australia, this signals the end of the unipolar era and the emergence of a new bipolar order where the US and China act as co-stewards of global stability, balancing competition with mutual necessity.
